prevalence.trend: Increasing due to aging population and lifestyle factors

vulnerablePopulations: Rural populations with limited healthcare access
Younger adults (under 45 years; higher burden in India with mean age of onset ~63 years)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on WHO criteria: rapid onset of focal neurological deficit lasting >24 hours or leading to death, confirmed by neuroimaging (CT/MRI) and clinical scales like NIHSS

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low (~20% for hypertension screening in primary care)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Poor; only 10-15% achieve blood pressure control

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Adapted WHO guidelines with emphasis on clinical diagnosis in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Follows Indian Stroke Guidelines (ISG); includes thrombolysis (rt-PA) within 4.5 hours, but access limited
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High younger stroke burden (20-25% under 40 years); urban-rural disparity; only 1-3% receive thrombolysis due to delayed presentation and infrastructure gaps
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Emerging stroke units in urban centers; government initiatives like Ayushman Bharat for coverage; increasing awareness campaigns
